content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 2, 2017

Lori Herbel
7518 Ventura Ave.
Yucca Valley, CA 92284

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-17-229

Dear Ms. Herbel:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.² Because your request seeks general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.³

Note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice. In addition, the Fair Political Practices Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective actions.

QUESTION

Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions preclude you from serving on a Town advisory commission in light of salary you have previously received as a contract instructor from the Town's community service department?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² In addition, the Town of Yucca Valley may impose restrictions on incompatible activities outside of the Act. You should consult the Town's counsel regarding any restrictions on incompatible activities the Town may impose.

³ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-17-229
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

Nothing in the Act prohibits you from serving on the advisory commission. Moreover, to the extent that you have an interest in the Town as a source of income and the advisory commission makes or participates in government decisions, you are only disqualified from decisions with a foreseeable material effect on the Town, a government entity, if there is a unique effect on your interest. (Regulation 18703(e)(7).) Accordingly, the Act does not limit your participation as a member of the advisory commission because there is no indication that actions by the advisory commission may have a unique effect on your salary as a contract instructor for the Town.

FACTS

You were appointed by The Yucca Valley Town Council to their Measure Y and Z Oversight Commission in April 2017. The Oversight Commission serves as an advisory commission to the Yucca Valley Town Council and members not receive any type of compensation, salary, or stipend. Both Measure Y and Z established a temporary half-percent general transactions and use tax. Measure Y funds are for essential services, and Measure Z funds are for a sewer assessment reduction. Additionally, for the past six years, you have received compensation as a contract instructor for a dog training course through the Town's Community Service Department.

ANALYSIS

There is nothing in the Act that precludes you from serving on the advisory commission notwithstanding your salary from the Town as a contract instructor. Moreover, the Act's conflict of interest provisions apply only to the extent that a committee, board, commission, group or other body has decision making authority. (See Regulation 18700(c)(2).)

While there is no indication that the advisory commission has decision making authority, assuming it does, the Act's conflict of interest provisions would prohibit you from taking part in governmental decisions in which you have a financial interest. (Section 87100.) An official has a financial interest in a decision only if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of the official's immediate family, or on any of the official's interests as specified in Section 87103.

As pertinent to this request, an official has an interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more received by, or promised to, the official within the 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) In this case, it does appear that you have an interest in the Town of Yucca Valley as a source of income.⁴ However, Regulation 18703(e)(7) provides that the financial effect on the governmental entity is deemed indistinguishable from the effect on the public generally if the

⁴ While the term "income" excludes salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a state, local or federal governmental agency (Section 82030(b)(2)), we have advised that compensation from a governmental agency constitutes salary that is exempted from the definition of "income" only when received as an employee or consultant of the agency. (Kohn Advice Letter, No. I-93-294.) The "governmental salary" exclusion does not apply to payments received as an independent contractor.

File No. I-17-229
Page No. 3

official establishes that the decision affects a "governmental entity in which the official has an
interest and there is no unique effect on the official's interest."³

Generally, your participation as a member of the advisory commission is not restricted
because there is no indication that decisions or recommendations of the advisory commission may
have a unique effect on your salary as a contract instructor for the Town. If, however, there is any
indication that a decision or recommendation by the advisory committee may affect your salary as a
contract instructor for the Town, you should seek additional advice.⁶

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Jack Woodside
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl

_______________

⁵ Notwithstanding the Act's disqualification rules, income received as a contract instructor is potentially
reportable income under the Act. If positions on the advisory commission have been designated in the Town's conflict
of interest code, you may be required to report the income on a statement of economic interests (Form 700). You will
need to consult the Town's conflict of interest code to determine if positions on the advisory commission are designated
positions and the types of interests that must be disclosed for the positions.

⁶ For example, if the advisory commission may recommend allocating measure funds to pay for or supplement
the community service program's dog training courses, there may be a unique effect on your interest and you should
seek additional advice.
